channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Crowds in Mayotte vent frustration with cyclone response as Macron tours devastation
item.description: MIRERENI, Mayotte (AP) — Crowds in Mayotte vented their frustration at French President Emmanuel Macron, with some booing, as he toured destruction wrought by the strongest cyclone to hit the French territory in nearly a century.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, ADRIENNE SURPRENANT and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Universities warn international students to come back to campus before Trump inauguration
item.description: More than a dozen U.S. universities have issued guidance to their international students urging them to be back on campus before the Jan. 20 inauguration of President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: What will Syria's future look like? The answer could lie in other Arab countries' recent pasts
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Even with hopes running high, so much can go wrong when a country ousts a longtime dictator and tries to start anew. The Middle Eastern and North African nations that attempted to transition to democracy in recent years can attest to that. Now it's Syria's turn to try to get it right.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Russian official says a Ukrainian strike with US-supplied missiles kills 6
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Ukrainian attack Friday on a town in Russia’s Kursk border region using U.S.-supplied missiles killed six people, including a child, a senior local official said.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chinese national charged with acting as Beijing's agent in local California election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Chinese national was arrested Thursday on charges of acting as an illegal agent for Beijing when serving as the campaign manager for an unnamed politician elected to a city council in Southern California two years ago.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, AMY TAXIN and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP seeks ‘maximum pressure’ on Iran with nuclear clock ticking
item.description: Republican lawmakers want a return to "maximum pressure" on Iran in the second Trump administration and are widely open to the idea of strikes on Iranian nuclear sites if it’s the only way to prevent Tehran from wielding nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: 8 dead, 18 rescued as speedboat carrying migrants capsizes near Greek island of Rhodes
item.description: ATHENS, Greeece (AP) — A speedboat carrying migrants capsized Friday morning off the eastern Greek island of Rhodes, leaving eight dead, Greek authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan lawmakers clash after party members break into legislature to occupy speaker's chair
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Lawmakers clashed in Taiwan's legislature Friday when members of a leading political party who had broken into the building overnight to occupy the speaker's chair tussled with members of another major party who forced their way in to evict them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: For thousands of Jews, Israel still doesn't feel safe after the Oct. 7 attacks. So they're leaving
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Leaving Israel is easier, Shira Z. Carmel thinks, by saying it's just for now. But she knows better. For the Israeli-born singer and an increasing number of relatively well-off Israelis, the Oct.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US diplomats in Damascus for first time in more than 10 years following fall of Assad regime
item.description: U.S. officials are in Syria with goals to advance Aqaba principles and seek information on American citizens who disappeared under the Assad regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU leaders insist no decisions can be taken about Ukraine without Ukraine, or behind their backs
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders insisted on Thursday that no decisions can be taken about the future of war-ravaged Ukraine without its consent — or behind the backs of its partners in Europe, barely a month before President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon doubled the number of US troops in Syria 'months' ahead of Assad’s fall
item.description: The U.S. for months has had more than 2,000 troops inside Syria, more than double the 900 troops the Defense Department previously said are on the ground there, the Pentagon’s top spokesperson revealed Thursday. The increased force presence has “been going on for a while,” with press secretary Maj.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Israel and Hamas appear close to a ceasefire deal. These are the sticking points
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel and Hamas appear closer than they have been in months to agreeing to a ceasefire that could wind down the 14-month war in Gaza and bring home dozens of people held hostage there. But the sides have come close before, only to have talks collapse over various disagreements.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 13 die as an Indian navy speedboat crashes into a passenger ferry off Mumbai
item.description: At least 13 people were killed after an Indian navy speedboat crashed into a ferry that was carrying more than 100 people; the ferry was on its way to a tourist destination off Mumbai.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blinken says 'pressure is on Hamas' to move forward with ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken said the pressure is on the Palestinian militant group Hamas to move forward with the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip, arguing the group’s support from its allies in the region has dwindled in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Putin ‘promises’ to ask Assad for help in finding Austin Tice following letter from mother
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin promised to ask former Syrian President Assad for help in obtaining information on American journalist Austin Tice after he was taken prisoner 12 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin says Russia ready to compromise with Trump on Ukraine war
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said he was ready to make a compromise regarding ending the war in Ukraine in possible talks with U.S. President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 takeaways from Putin's annual press conference
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin talked for more than three hours during an annual news conference in Moscow on Thursday, discussing key topics including the war in Ukraine, his interest in dialogue with President-elect Trump and an openness to asking about detained journalist Austin Tice in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Frenchman found guilty in horrific rape trial that shocked the world
item.description: A Frenchman accused of drugging his wife of 50 years and inviting strangers over to rape her – while he filmed the assaults – was found guilty on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia detains suspect accused of killing high-ranking general in Moscow
item.description: A suspect has been taken into custody in connection with the Tuesday killing of Lt. Gen. Igor Kirillov and his assistant in Moscow, according to Russian officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blast kills 2 Mexican soldiers and wounds 5 others in suspected booby trap by drug cartel
item.description: While approaching, they spotted three dismembered bodies near the encampment, which appeared to be abandoned. But as they drew closer, a buried device exploded and struck the soldiers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canada's finance minister resigns as unpopular Trudeau faces biggest test of his political career
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau faced the biggest test of his political career after Finance Minister Chrystia Freeland, long one of his most powerful and loyal ministers, resigned from the Cabinet on Monday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainian official takes credit after Russian general Igor Kirillov killed by explosive device in Moscow
item.description: An official with the Ukrainian Security Services says the agency was behind the bombing assassination of Lt. Gen. Igor Kirillov, commander of Russia’s chemical, biological and radiation defense forces, and his assistant in Moscow on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FIFA apologizes to Ukraine soccer body for Crimea map error at 2026 World Cup draw
item.description: “While the incident was unintentional, we sincerely regret any concern it may have cause,” FIFA’s chief member association officer wrote in a letter .
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cartel-run clandestine fentanyl labs increasingly difficult to track
item.description: Mexican drug cartels are driving an opioid epidemic, flooding the United States with increasingly potent synthetic drugs.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea’s parliament votes to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his martial law order
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s parliament on Saturday impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol over his stunning and short-lived martial law decree, a move that ended days of political paralysis but set up an intense debate over Yoon's fate, as jubilant crowds roared to celebrate another defiant m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-soccer player Kavelashvili becomes Georgia's president in a blow to country's EU aspirations
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Former soccer player Mikheil Kavelashvili became president of Georgia on Saturday, as the ruling party tightened its grip in what the opposition calls a blow to the country’s EU aspirations and a victory for former imperial ruler Russia.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US officials have been in direct contact with the Syrian rebel group that ousted Assad, Blinken says
item.description: AQABA, Jordan (AP) — American officials have been in direct contact with the terrorist-designated rebel group that led the overthrow of Syrian President Bashar Assad, U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken said Saturday. Blinken, speaking at a news conference in Jordan, was the first U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Woman arrested smuggling pounds of meth wrapped as a Christmas gift
item.description: Customs officers arrested a traveler who investigators say tried to smuggle pounds of methamphetamine disguised as a Christmas gift into New Zealand last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Syrians celebrate Assad's fall as US seeks a peaceful political transition
item.description: Thousands of Syrians gathered in Damascus’ main square and a historic mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since former President Bashar Assad was overthrown, a major symbolic moment for the country’s dramatic change of power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US military flies American released from Syrian prison to Jordan, officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military has transported out of Syria an American who had disappeared seven months ago into former President Bashar Assad’s notorious prison system and was among the thousands released this week by rebels, U.S. officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation
